title: Xtra Garlicky Spanish Pork Jamon Iberico
description: A garlicky and pillowy Spanish dish featuring turmeric powder, spherified to resemble Jamon Iberico, cooked in a tandoor clay pot. This Whole30-friendly recipe is a quick fix for a delicious Andalusian meal.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender, combine garlic, turmeric powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper to make a spice blend.
Add the spice blend to the pork shoulder and mix well.
Heat the olive oil in the tandoor clay pot and sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides.
Add the water to the pot and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix the sodium alginate with a little water to dissolve, then add to the pot and stir gently.
Let the mixture cook for 10 minutes, allowing the sodium alginate to spherify the pork mixture.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly before serving.
To serve, scoop the spherified pork mixture into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
